perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall ensure (i) that all persons mentio ned in Paragraph 1.2.2 above are made aware, prior to any disclosure, of the confidential nature of th e Confidential Information and of the contents of t his Agreement and (ii) [that such persons are bound by confidentially with respect to the Confidential Information as If they were a party to this Agreeme nt.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party shall ensure (i) that all persons mentio ned in Paragraph 1.2.2 above are made aware, after any disclosure, of the confidential nature of th e Confidential Information and of the contents of t his Agreement and (ii) [that such persons are bound by confidentially with respect to the Confidential Information as If they were a party to this Agreeme nt.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'prior to any disclosure' to 'after any disclosure', the agreement now instructs parties to inform individuals of confidentiality AFTER information has already been disclosed. This contradicts the core principle of an NDA, which is to protect information BEFORE it is shared. The original definition of Confidential Information as well as obligations to safeguard it becomes contradictory, creating uncertainty if informing individuals after disclosure can actually safeguard it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Parties’ making available t o each other Confidential Information as defined in Sub- clause 1.1, each Party hereby agrees to the followi ng confidentiality undertakings for and on behalf of itself and for and on behalf of the Party’s employees, adv isors and representatives: • All Confidential Information received by a Party sh all be held in complete confidence by such Party and by the Party’s directors, employees, advisors o r representatives, and shall not without the prior written consent of the other Party be used for any purpose other than in connection with the purpose for which such Confidential Information was disclos ed or received. • A Party shall not disclose any Confidential Informa tion received by it to any person other than those of Party’s employees, advisors and representatives who are directly concerned with the purpose for which such Confidential Information was disclosed o r received and who need to know such Confidential Information. • Each Party shall ensure (i) that all persons mentio ned in Paragraph 1.2.2 above are made aware, after any disclosure, of the confidential nature of th e Confidential Information and of the contents of t his Agreement and (ii) [that such persons are bound by confidentially with respect to the Confidential Information as If they were a party to this Agreeme nt.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'prior to any disclosure' to 'after any disclosure' directly contradicts the principle of protecting Confidential Information before it is shared. The Contradicted Text outlines confidentiality undertakings based on the premise that confidential information is safeguarded upon receipt. Informing individuals of confidentiality AFTER disclosure fundamentally undermines these undertakings and creates a strong, direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties shall have in effect and maintain adequ ate security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information from unauthorized access, disclosure, u se, and/or misappropriation.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties shall have in effect and maintain minimal security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information from unauthorized access, disclosure, u se, and/or misappropriation.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'adequate security measures' to 'minimal security measures', the clause now contradicts the purpose of an NDA. While it requires security, the term 'minimal' is vague and subjective. This undermines the enforceability of the confidentiality obligation, as 'minimal' may not be sufficient to genuinely protect the information as referred in clause 1.1. It creates an uncertainty about the level of protection the confidential information would get from the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Parties’ making available t o each other Confidential Information as defined in Sub- clause 1.1, each Party hereby agrees to the followi ng confidentiality undertakings for and on behalf of itself and for and on behalf of the Party’s employees, adv isors and representatives: • All Confidential Information received by a Party sh all be held in complete confidence by such Party and by the Party’s directors, employees, advisors o r representatives, and shall not without the prior written consent of the other Party be used for any purpose other than in connection with the purpose for which such Confidential Information was disclos ed or received. • A Party shall not disclose any Confidential Informa tion received by it to any person other than those of Party’s employees, advisors and representatives who are directly concerned with the purpose for which such Confidential Information was disclosed o r received and who need to know such Confidential Information. • Each Party shall ensure (i) that all persons mentio ned in Paragraph 1.2.2 above are made aware, prior to any disclosure, of the confidential nature of th e Confidential Information and of the contents of t his Agreement and (ii) [that such persons are bound by confidentially with respect to the Confidential Information as If they were a party to this Agreeme nt. • The Parties shall have in effect and maintain minimal security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information from unauthorized access, disclosure, u se, and/or misappropriation.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires 'adequate security measures', while the changed text mandates only 'minimal security measures'. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text in this case, uses this modified clause. This creates a contradiction because 'minimal' is subjective and potentially insufficient to protect confidential information as intended by the NDA, undermining the agreement's enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding Sub-clause 1.1, the obligations und ertaken by a Party under this Agreement to maintain confidentiality shall not apply to the extent that the Party is or becomes under an obligation to disc lose Confidential Information (i) by order of a court of competent jurisdiction, or (ii) under statutory la w including according to The Danish Access to Public Administration Files Act, provided that where possi ble the Party shall first notify the other Party of suc h obligation and upon request allow the other Party to advance any defense against such obligation where a ppropriate.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding Sub-clause 1.1, the obligations und ertaken by a Party under this Agreement to maintain confidentiality shall not apply to the extent that the Party is or becomes under an obligation to disc lose Confidential Information (i) by order of a court of competent jurisdiction, or (ii) under statutory la w including according to The Danish Access to Public Administration Files Act, provided that the Party shall first notify the other Party of suc h obligation and deny the other Party to advance any defense against such obligation where a ppropriate.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'allow the other Party to advance any defense' to 'deny the other Party to advance any defense' directly contradicts the idea of collaboration in protecting confidential information. It creates an obligation to inform, but simultaneously removes the right to defend, making the notification requirement meaningless in terms of actual protection. This also contradicts the obligations mentioned in sub-clause 1.2 in which states that each party needs to maintain adequate security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding Sub-clause 1.1, the obligations und ertaken by a Party under this Agreement to maintain confidentiality shall not apply to the extent that the Party is or becomes under an obligation to disc lose Confidential Information (i) by order of a court of competent jurisdiction, or (ii) under statutory la w including according to The Danish Access to Public Administration Files Act, provided that the Party shall first notify the other Party of suc h obligation and deny the other Party to advance any defense against such obligation where a ppropriate. In any event, a Party shall notify the other Party of any disclosure made under this Sub-clause 1.3 promptly after disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by changing 'allow the other Party to advance any defense' to 'deny the other Party to advance any defense'. This creates a direct conflict within the document. The original text allows for collaboration in protecting confidential information, while the changed text prevents it. This contradicts the broader understanding of the contract related to protecting confidential information and sub-clause 1.2. Because the modified text creates a clear and direct conflict with the contradicted text, the response is YES.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall be liable in accordance with Danis h law for damages (including claims, expenses and indirect losses of whatsoever nature) arising out o f or in connection with that Party’s breach of its obligations under this Agreement, without prejudice to other rights and remedies available including, but not limited to, restrictive injunction and other eq uitable relief.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party shall be liable in accordance with Danis h law for damages (including claims, expenses and indirect losses of whatsoever nature) arising out o f or in connection with that Party’s breach of its obligations under this Agreement, prejudice to other rights and remedies available including, but not limited to, restrictive injunction and other eq uitable relief.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'without' the clause now states that the party is liable WITH prejudice to other rights and remedies, this implies that by damaging one party, it restricts its rights and remedies. Therefore it contradicts with the idea of free will of a party to chose any right and remedy available.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party shall be liable in accordance with Danis h law for damages (including claims, expenses and indirect losses of whatsoever nature) arising out o f or in connection with that Party’s breach of its obligations under this Agreement, prejudice to other rights and remedies available including, but not limited to, restrictive injunction and other eq uitable relief.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'without prejudice to other rights and remedies', meaning a party's liability does not restrict their other rights and remedies. The changed text removes 'without', so it now reads 'prejudice to other rights and remedies'. This implies that a party's liability DOES restrict their other rights and remedies, directly contradicting the concept of free will for choosing available remedies. Since the Changed Text and the Contradicted text are in the same location, but the change causes a contradiction as explained above, this is a clear in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, as a single clause cannot support both meanings concurrently.

perturbation.original_text: A Party’s payment of damages or the application of any other remedies shall not release such Party fr om its obligations of confidentiality as set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: A Party’s payment of damages or the application of any other remedies shall release such Party fr om its obligations of confidentiality as set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'not' in this section, this is saying that once a party pays for the damages, it will then be released from its confidentiality obligations. This directly contradicts the purpose of an NDA, this undermines the confidentiality and intellectual property rights provisions as the violating party is now free from keeping confidentiality
perturbation.contradicted_text: A Party’s payment of damages or the application of any other remedies shall release such Party fr om its obligations of confidentiality as set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that payment of damages does *not* release a party from confidentiality obligations. The changed text removes the 'not,' stating that payment *does* release a party from those obligations. Because the contradicted text is the same as the changed text, this means the text contradicts what the original text says regarding confidentiality and the consequences of the payment of damages. Based on the Perturbation Explanation, it explicitly states that removing 'not' creates contradiction with NDA's purpose.
